holdings.reason: Four-lens consensus with zero dissent is the highest conviction signal in the slate. Hawkish hold NIM tailwind is structurally intact and not consensus-priced; SpaceX IPO halo plus dealmaking renaissance provide independent catalysts even if IPO timing slips. RS +24 vs SPY with RSI 61 is clean momentum with room to extend. Maximum weight is earned — this is what maximum weight is for.

ETF structure is the correct vehicle for AI-capex secular exposure — eliminates binary single-name risk while capturing the dominant sector momentum regime. Confirmed 52wk-high breakout, RS +52, RSI 59 not extended. Three-analyst consensus, zero dissent. SMH is the semiconductor anchor; its full weight is what justifies cutting the single-name stacks below it.

Genuine diversifier — non-correlated to semis, rates, and mega-cap tech. Six institutional upgrades in two weeks mark a narrative inflection from utilization crisis to margin recovery with re-rating in early innings. RS +32.5, RSI 64 warm but not overbought, three analysts with zero dissent. June 23 dividend provides near-term sentiment anchor.

RSI 34 on a rotation-driven 10.6% pullback is a credible dislocation signal for a franchise with durable Search + YouTube + Cloud moats; Gemini deepens AI positioning. Sized at 7% to respect live Technicals dissent on mega-cap tech broadly. Adds genuine diversification with zero overlap to semis, financials, or healthcare. Toe-in sizing is the right call pending a technical base.

RS +152 is the loudest momentum signal in the book but the 5d +9.7% run makes full-size a recency chase. Half-weight participates in semi cycle acceleration without compounding sub-sector concentration — SMH already holds AMD as a top constituent. Momentum override of Fundamentals valuation caution is acceptable at half-size; at full-size it becomes undisciplined.

thesis: The book runs three independent macro themes with clean sizing: rates-driven financial recovery (GS 15%), AI-capex secular demand via ETF anchor plus one high-beta complement (SMH 15% + AMD 6%), and healthcare margin normalization (UNH 12%), with a small toe-in to a technically dislocated mega-cap franchise (GOOGL 7%). The Risk Manager correctly diagnosed the semiconductor stacking problem — SMH + AMD + MU at 35% was one thesis expressed three times with two names coming off parabolic moves; MU is dropped entirely and AMD halved, bringing semi exposure to a disciplined 21% with SMH doing the heavy lifting. 45% cash is not timidity — it is the bid we reserve for positions that earn the same multi-analyst conviction bar the current book required.
note: Four-lens GS and clean-breakout SMH do the heavy lifting. We do not buy names on five-day parabolas backed by a single analyst — that is how you confuse noise for signal.
outlook: Three catalysts to watch: (1) Fed June meeting tone — a dovish tilt pressures the GS NIM thesis and tests whether SMH's breakout sustains without the rates tailwind; (2) GOOGL technical stabilization — any reversal above the 50-day on volume validates adding toward 10%; (3) semi earnings revisions — if AMD or MU forward estimates are cut on the next analyst cycle, trim AMD to 3% and reassess SMH weight. MU sits at zero weight on the watchlist; a multi-analyst re-rating backed by confirmed memory-cycle inflection data would be the entry signal, not a single-day gap.

weakness_watch.reason: 5d +9.7% is the book's highest near-term reversal risk. If RSI pushes above 70 or SMH shows distribution, AMD is the first trim — it is the most redundant position in the slate and the one sized on recency momentum.

Technicals dissent is live and RSI 34 can become RSI 20 if the mega-cap rotation deepens. Monitor for a base formation — absence of stabilization above a key level would warrant exiting before thesis damage compounds.

RSI 64 and only 1.1% off 52wk high. If the institutional upgrade cycle stalls or utilization data disappoints pre-earnings, this becomes the healthcare fade trade. June 23 dividend is a sentiment floor, not a price floor.

SpaceX IPO timing is speculative and any Fed pivot signal compresses NIM expectations directly. A dovish surprise would require re-evaluating the primary thesis even if the dealmaking narrative holds independently.

pitches.thesis: Hawkish hold at 3.50-3.75% with Dec hike risk being priced in is the single biggest NIM and capital-markets tailwind for the most rate-levered major bank; RS +24 vs SPY and clean RSI 61 give room to run.
Higher-for-longer confirmed at this week's FOMC directly fattens bank spreads; JPM's diversified platform is the safest vehicle for the financials macro trade in a 1.7%-GDP slow-growth regime.

CBO and Treasury data explicitly name AI capex as the primary US GDP growth driver in 2026; semi ETF captures that structural demand regardless of rate cycle, with RS +52 and RSI 59 — not yet overbought.

Fiscal spend from the 2025 reconciliation act is flowing into infrastructure and defense procurement; CAT is the cleanest industrial proxy for government capex with RS +19 and RSI 55 — plenty of room.

weak.reason: Hawkish hold + firm DXY ~99 + Dec hike risk being priced = textbook gold headwind; rate cuts are off the table through 2026, removing gold's primary macro bid. RS -28 and -22% off highs confirm.
Fed signaling hawkish hold with potential December hike priced by markets pushes long-end yields up; long-duration bonds are structurally offside in this regime.

3.8% inflation + zero rate relief = consumer discretionary spending under maximum pressure; RSI 15 is extreme but a broken trend in a high-cost-of-living macro environment has no floor from Fed support.
Crypto's primary macro catalyst was a Fed pivot — with that gone and geopolitical safe-haven flows favouring USD not risk assets, the bid evaporates. RS -33 and -65% off highs are the macro verdict.
Same as ETHA: hawkish hold kills the liquidity-premium narrative that drove crypto through 2024-25; DXY firm at 99 is the opposing force.
Consumer under pressure from 3.8% headline inflation + high rates; AWS cloud growth faces enterprise budget scrutiny in a 1.7% GDP, 30%-recession-risk environment. RSI 27, -11.7% in one month.

pitches.note: The defining macro force this week is the FOMC hawkish hold — rates at 3.50-3.75% with a December hike being priced, core CPI at 2.9%, and no pivot in sight. That regime clearly favours rate-sensitive financials (GS, JPM) and structural AI capex plays (SMH) while crushing long-duration (TLT), gold (GLD), and consumer discretionary. Middle East geopolitical risk adds oil upside optionality but XLE price action is not confirming, so I am sidestepping energy entirely.

pitches.thesis: Search + YouTube + Cloud franchise with structurally durable earnings; RSI 34 on a modest 10.6% pullback while Gemini deepens the moat — rotation-driven dislocation, not fundamental impairment.
AWS leadership + accelerating advertising revenue + Prime flywheel; RSI 27 flags near-term dislocation in a compounder whose earnings power is structurally improving quarter over quarter.

50.7% off 52-week high is an extraordinary discount for a subscription software franchise with 90%+ gross margins and deep enterprise lock-in; Firefly positions Adobe as an AI beneficiary, not a casualty — RSI 33 and a 19% 5-day flush hint at capitulation.

43.5% off high while OCI is winning AI infrastructure contracts and Autonomous Database + NetSuite SaaS provide a durable earnings floor — the re-rating catalyst is AI workload monetization, and the market is ignoring it.

weak.reason: RSI 15 with a 40% drawdown and -15.7% in 3 months signals fundamental deterioration, not just a technical flush — advertising tier monetization or subscriber trajectory may be structurally missing expectations.
Linear TV secular decline accelerating, streaming growth plateauing after initial gains, theme park pricing power approaching ceiling — no credible near-term earnings acceleration thesis.
Multi-quarter revenue decline, China structurally challenged, turnaround under new CEO is real but measured in years — market is pricing hope, not results.
Copilot enterprise monetization running behind consensus expectations, Azure growth decelerating against tough comps — down 27.5% from high in a down trend with no clear near-term re-rating catalyst.
Minority view flagged alongside my pitch: if Firefly cannibalization of paid seat count is worse than modeled, the AI beneficiary thesis collapses — worth monitoring.

pitches.note: The best fundamental value is in quality internet/software — GOOGL and AMZN offer durable earnings power at oversold levels while ADBE represents a classic franchise-in-dislocation setup. Semiconductors (AMD, MU) have already re-rated sharply and offer less value cushion from this lens.

pitches.thesis: SpaceX IPO halo (lead underwriter, largest IPO in US history at $75B raised) cements 'dealmaking renaissance' narrative; sentiment still building with RSI 61 and only -2.7% off 52wk high, yet 3m RS vs SPY +24.2 shows institutional rotation isn't done.

Six institutional upgrades in two weeks signal a narrative inflection from 'utilization crisis' to 'margin recovery and AI efficiency' — that re-rating is still in early innings; June 23 dividend acts as near-term sentiment anchor.

ByteDance AI chip supply deal plus imminent hyperscaler custom processor shipment are undercovered incremental catalysts; stock has pulled back 15% from its recent peak to RSI 42 — sentiment reset creates a re-entry window before the next catalyst confirms.

RSI 15 and seven consecutive down sessions with 0 analyst sell ratings and a $114 consensus vs $80 price point signals capitulation, not a fundamental collapse — bad news (failed WBD deal, Brazil tax, Q2 guide miss) is fully priced; contrarian bounce only.

weak.reason: CFO departed June 15, stock hit 52wk lows post-earnings despite a beat — 'AI eats creative software' narrative fully owns the story now and there is no positive counter-catalyst visible.
Three-year lows driven by the same legacy-software-disrupted-by-AI narrative as ADBE; AI-driven workforce reductions feeding the bear story, no sentiment inflection point in sight.
Down 27.5% from highs with RSI 39 despite 40% Azure growth — market is repricing AI capex-skepticism and Copilot monetization lag; sentiment has decoupled from fundamentals in the wrong direction.
Risk-on regime plus higher-for-longer rates (10y at 4.54%) actively squeezing the gold narrative; -22% off highs with 1m -10.2% and RSI 33 — no sentiment catalyst on the horizon.
Crypto malaise with no positive headline catalyst; RSI 22, -49% off high, down trend — sentiment is absent, not just negative.

pitches.note: The clearest sentiment pocket in the market right now is financials — GS has a generational headline halo and BAC/JPM are riding a credible NII-recovery story into the first FOMC under Warsh (today). Tech is deeply bifurcated: AI infrastructure (AMD/QCOM/SMH) is rebounding on real deal flow while legacy software (ADBE/CRM/MSFT) has no floor; the most asymmetric setup in the book is NFLX at RSI 15 — not a high-conviction long, but the crowd has fully given up and that is usually worth fading for a short-term bounce.

3m RS +118 vs SPY with 5d +13.6% re-acceleration — memory cycle inflecting hard; RSI 64 warm but not extended enough to trim.
Semiconductor ETF at 52wk high, RS +52 vs SPY, RSI 59 — cleanest broad-beta vehicle to ride the dominant momentum regime without single-name binary risk.
Financials cross-sectional leader: 3m RS +24 vs SPY, only -2.7% off ATH, RSI 61 — financials rotation intact and GS leads the group.
Surprise 3m RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, -1.1% off ATH with confirmed uptrend — healthcare's lone momentum standout, minimal drawdown risk.

weak.reason: RSI 15, 3m RS -28.0, down trend, -40% off 52wk high — momentum rank dead last among comms; no reversal signal.
3m RS -30.5, RSI 33, -50.7% off high, down trend — structural breakdown, not a mean-reversion candidate at this stage.
Worst drawdown in universe at -65.6% off high, RSI 23, 3m RS -33.2 — crypto capitulation ongoing, no momentum floor visible.
3m RS -25.8, -38.9% off high, down trend, RSI 41 — enterprise SaaS broken; ORCL confirms sector-wide SaaS rotation out.
3m RS -28.4, RSI 33, 1m -10.2% — intermediate support failing; gold momentum rank among the lowest in universe.
3m RS -13.3, RSI 39, down trend, -27.5% off high — mega-cap tech that's visibly underperforming its own sector.

pitches.note: Semiconductor momentum is the single clearest cross-sectional regime signal this cycle — AMD and MU rank 1-2 by 3m RS by a factor of 3x over the next best name; SMH packages that in ETF form. Secondary rotation in Financials (GS). Weakness is concentrated in mega-cap software (ADBE, CRM, MSFT) and crypto (ETHA, IBIT) — the distribution of RS scores is unusually wide, signaling a strong factor dispersion environment that favors pure momentum longs.

cio.note: Four analysts converge on GS (highest confluence this cycle) and three each on SMH and UNH, anchoring those three at full or near-full weight; AMD earns 12% as the #1 cross-sectional momentum name despite only two champions. The 31% cash reserve is deliberate — GOOGL and MU are sized defensively as single-analyst ideas, and the hawkish-hold + semis-leadership regime does not reward adding speculative or technically broken names (ADBE, ORCL, QCOM) with no price confirmation. No longs in long-duration, gold, crypto, or consumer discretionary — Macro's regime call is clean and all other lenses align with it.

slate.thesis: Widest analyst confluence in the book: hawkish hold at 3.50-3.75% is a direct NIM tailwind, SpaceX IPO halo cements the dealmaking renaissance narrative, RS +24 vs SPY with RSI 61 shows healthy momentum with room to run, and Quant ranks it as the cross-sectional financials leader. Four independent lenses all pointing the same direction — maximum weight.

AI capex is the primary 2026 GDP growth driver per CBO/Treasury data; SMH at 52wk high with RS +52 and RSI 59 is a confirmed breakout, not yet overbought. ETF structure avoids single-name binary risk while capturing the dominant sector momentum regime. Three analysts (Macro structural demand + Technicals breakout + Quant regime signal) with no dissent.

Six institutional upgrades in two weeks signal narrative inflection from utilization crisis to margin recovery — re-rating still in early innings. RS +32.5, only 1.1% off 52wk high, RSI 64; healthcare's lone momentum standout with June 23 dividend acting as a near-term sentiment anchor. Three analysts, zero dissent.

RS +152 vs SPY is the single loudest momentum signal in the book — roughly 3x the next-best name. RSI 57 neutral with 5d +9.7% continuation confirms the semis cycle is in full acceleration, not late-stage. High-beta single-name complement to SMH; Fundamentals caution on stretched valuations is noted but momentum regime overrides valuation in this environment.

RS +118 and 5d +13.6% re-acceleration signal a hard memory cycle inflection; Quant ranks it #2 cross-sectionally. RSI 64 is warm but not extended enough to trim. Sized to 8% — not 12%+ — because Fundamentals warns semis have already re-rated sharply, and this is a single-analyst pitch. Adds cycle-specific exposure SMH ETF underweights.

Structurally durable Search + YouTube + Cloud franchise at RSI 34 on a rotation-driven 10.6% pullback — Fundamentals diagnoses dislocation, not impairment. Gemini deepens the moat. Sized small (7%) because Technicals explicitly flagged mega-cap tech as broadly broken and neither Quant nor Sentiment championed it; a toe-in position pending technical stabilization.

verdicts.reason: Four-lens confluence with zero dissent, RSI 61 healthy, NIM tailwind thesis structurally intact and not consensus-priced yet. SpaceX IPO timing is speculative but the dealmaking renaissance narrative has independent legs. Max weight is earned by conviction depth here.

Cleanest semiconductor expression in the book: ETF structure eliminates binary single-name risk, 52wk-high breakout confirmed, RSI 59 is not extended. Three-analyst consensus, no dissent. Keeping SMH at full weight is precisely WHY the single-name semi stacks below it can be sized down — this is the anchor.

Genuinely non-correlated to the semis and macro-rates themes. RSI 64 is warm but not overbought, institutional upgrade cycle is early-innings per the thesis, three analysts with zero dissent. June 23 dividend provides near-term sentiment floor. No objections.

5d +9.7% is the loudest recency-chasing signal in the book. RS +152 is impressive but SMH already holds AMD as a top constituent, so full 12% meaningfully double-counts the same bet. Fundamentals flagged stretched valuations and got overridden by momentum — that override is acceptable at half-size, not full. Six percent participates in the semi cycle without compounding sub-sector concentration unnecessarily.

Single-analyst pitch (Quant only) carrying the largest 5-day run in the entire slate (+13.6%) — this is chasing an already-moved name, not entering a fresh thesis. Fundamentals explicitly warned against semis re-rating and was not overridden here because no other analyst stepped up. SMH already captures MU through its ETF holdings, so this adds correlation without adding conviction. At full slate, SMH+AMD+MU = 35% in one sub-sector riding the same AI-capex/memory-cycle thesis — that is a single-factor bet masquerading as three positions. MU is the weakest link and gets cut.

At 7% the downside is contained and the position is sized to reflect exactly the technical uncertainty. RSI 34 on a rotation-driven pullback is a credible dislocation signal for a franchise of this durability — Fundamentals is correct that this is not impairment. Non-correlated to semis, financials, or healthcare; adds genuine diversification to the slate. Toe-in sizing is appropriate given Technicals dissent.

risk.summary: The book's structural problem is semiconductor sub-sector stacking: SMH + AMD + MU = 35% in effectively one thesis (AI capex / memory cycle inflection) expressed three times with high cross-correlation and two names coming off extreme 5-day runs. Dropping MU (thinnest thesis, hottest recent move, fully redundant via SMH) and halving AMD (recency chase on top of SMH overlap) brings semiconductor exposure to ~21% with SMH doing the heavy lifting cleanly — no sector-limit breach, no chasing signal, and the AI-capex thesis remains fully represented. The remainder of the book — GS, UNH, GOOGL — is well-constructed with genuine theme diversification and appropriate sizing, and the revised slate lands at ~55% gross with 45% cash buffer.

Six institutional upgrades in two weeks signal a narrative inflection from 'utilization crisis' to 'margin recovery and AI efficiency' — that re-rating is still in early innings; June 23 dividend acts as near-term sentiment anchor.

ByteDance AI chip supply deal plus imminent hyperscaler custom processor shipment are undercovered incremental catalysts; stock has pulled back 15% from its recent peak to RSI 42 — sentiment reset creates a re-entry window before the next catalyst confirms.

RSI 15 and seven consecutive down sessions with 0 analyst sell ratings and a $114 consensus vs $80 price point signals capitulation, not a fundamental collapse — bad news (failed WBD deal, Brazil tax, Q2 guide miss) is fully priced; contrarian bounce only.

weak.reason: CFO departed June 15, stock hit 52wk lows post-earnings despite a beat — 'AI eats creative software' narrative fully owns the story now and there is no positive counter-catalyst visible.
Three-year lows driven by the same legacy-software-disrupted-by-AI narrative as ADBE; AI-driven workforce reductions feeding the bear story, no sentiment inflection point in sight.
Down 27.5% from highs with RSI 39 despite 40% Azure growth — market is repricing AI capex-skepticism and Copilot monetization lag; sentiment has decoupled from fundamentals in the wrong direction.
Risk-on regime plus higher-for-longer rates (10y at 4.54%) actively squeezing the gold narrative; -22% off highs with 1m -10.2% and RSI 33 — no sentiment catalyst on the horizon.
Crypto malaise with no positive headline catalyst; RSI 22, -49% off high, down trend — sentiment is absent, not just negative.

pitches.note: The clearest sentiment pocket in the market right now is financials — GS has a generational headline halo and BAC/JPM are riding a credible NII-recovery story into the first FOMC under Warsh (today). Tech is deeply bifurcated: AI infrastructure (AMD/QCOM/SMH) is rebounding on real deal flow while legacy software (ADBE/CRM/MSFT) has no floor; the most asymmetric setup in the book is NFLX at RSI 15 — not a high-conviction long, but the crowd has fully given up and that is usually worth fading for a short-term bounce.

pitches.thesis: Cross-sectional momentum leader by widest margin: 3m RS +152 vs SPY, RSI 57 leaves room to extend, fresh 5d +9.7% confirms continuation — semis cycle in full acceleration.
3m RS +118 vs SPY with 5d +13.6% re-acceleration — memory cycle inflecting hard; RSI 64 warm but not extended enough to trim.
Semiconductor ETF at 52wk high, RS +52 vs SPY, RSI 59 — cleanest broad-beta vehicle to ride the dominant momentum regime without single-name binary risk.
Financials cross-sectional leader: 3m RS +24 vs SPY, only -2.7% off ATH, RSI 61 — financials rotation intact and GS leads the group.
Surprise 3m RS +32.5 vs SPY, RSI 64, -1.1% off ATH with confirmed uptrend — healthcare's lone momentum standout, minimal drawdown risk.

weak.reason: RSI 15, 3m RS -28.0, down trend, -40% off 52wk high — momentum rank dead last among comms; no reversal signal.
3m RS -30.5, RSI 33, -50.7% off high, down trend — structural breakdown, not a mean-reversion candidate at this stage.
Worst drawdown in universe at -65.6% off high, RSI 23, 3m RS -33.2 — crypto capitulation ongoing, no momentum floor visible.
3m RS -25.8, -38.9% off high, down trend, RSI 41 — enterprise SaaS broken; ORCL confirms sector-wide SaaS rotation out.
3m RS -28.4, RSI 33, 1m -10.2% — intermediate support failing; gold momentum rank among the lowest in universe.
3m RS -13.3, RSI 39, down trend, -27.5% off high — mega-cap tech that's visibly underperforming its own sector.

pitches.note: Semiconductor momentum is the single clearest cross-sectional regime signal this cycle — AMD and MU rank 1-2 by 3m RS by a factor of 3x over the next best name; SMH packages that in ETF form. Secondary rotation in Financials (GS). Weakness is concentrated in mega-cap software (ADBE, CRM, MSFT) and crypto (ETHA, IBIT) — the distribution of RS scores is unusually wide, signaling a strong factor dispersion environment that favors pure momentum longs.

cio.note: Four analysts converge on GS (highest confluence this cycle) and three each on SMH and UNH, anchoring those three at full or near-full weight; AMD earns 12% as the #1 cross-sectional momentum name despite only two champions. The 31% cash reserve is deliberate — GOOGL and MU are sized defensively as single-analyst ideas, and the hawkish-hold + semis-leadership regime does not reward adding speculative or technically broken names (ADBE, ORCL, QCOM) with no price confirmation. No longs in long-duration, gold, crypto, or consumer discretionary — Macro's regime call is clean and all other lenses align with it.

slate.thesis: Widest analyst confluence in the book: hawkish hold at 3.50-3.75% is a direct NIM tailwind, SpaceX IPO halo cements the dealmaking renaissance narrative, RS +24 vs SPY with RSI 61 shows healthy momentum with room to run, and Quant ranks it as the cross-sectional financials leader. Four independent lenses all pointing the same direction — maximum weight.

AI capex is the primary 2026 GDP growth driver per CBO/Treasury data; SMH at 52wk high with RS +52 and RSI 59 is a confirmed breakout, not yet overbought. ETF structure avoids single-name binary risk while capturing the dominant sector momentum regime. Three analysts (Macro structural demand + Technicals breakout + Quant regime signal) with no dissent.

Six institutional upgrades in two weeks signal narrative inflection from utilization crisis to margin recovery — re-rating still in early innings. RS +32.5, only 1.1% off 52wk high, RSI 64; healthcare's lone momentum standout with June 23 dividend acting as a near-term sentiment anchor. Three analysts, zero dissent.

RS +152 vs SPY is the single loudest momentum signal in the book — roughly 3x the next-best name. RSI 57 neutral with 5d +9.7% continuation confirms the semis cycle is in full acceleration, not late-stage. High-beta single-name complement to SMH; Fundamentals caution on stretched valuations is noted but momentum regime overrides valuation in this environment.

RS +118 and 5d +13.6% re-acceleration signal a hard memory cycle inflection; Quant ranks it #2 cross-sectionally. RSI 64 is warm but not extended enough to trim. Sized to 8% — not 12%+ — because Fundamentals warns semis have already re-rated sharply, and this is a single-analyst pitch. Adds cycle-specific exposure SMH ETF underweights.

Structurally durable Search + YouTube + Cloud franchise at RSI 34 on a rotation-driven 10.6% pullback — Fundamentals diagnoses dislocation, not impairment. Gemini deepens the moat. Sized small (7%) because Technicals explicitly flagged mega-cap tech as broadly broken and neither Quant nor Sentiment championed it; a toe-in position pending technical stabilization.

verdicts.reason: Four-lens confluence with zero dissent, RSI 61 healthy, NIM tailwind thesis structurally intact and not consensus-priced yet. SpaceX IPO timing is speculative but the dealmaking renaissance narrative has independent legs. Max weight is earned by conviction depth here.

Cleanest semiconductor expression in the book: ETF structure eliminates binary single-name risk, 52wk-high breakout confirmed, RSI 59 is not extended. Three-analyst consensus, no dissent. Keeping SMH at full weight is precisely WHY the single-name semi stacks below it can be sized down — this is the anchor.

Genuinely non-correlated to the semis and macro-rates themes. RSI 64 is warm but not overbought, institutional upgrade cycle is early-innings per the thesis, three analysts with zero dissent. June 23 dividend provides near-term sentiment floor. No objections.

5d +9.7% is the loudest recency-chasing signal in the book. RS +152 is impressive but SMH already holds AMD as a top constituent, so full 12% meaningfully double-counts the same bet. Fundamentals flagged stretched valuations and got overridden by momentum — that override is acceptable at half-size, not full. Six percent participates in the semi cycle without compounding sub-sector concentration unnecessarily.

Single-analyst pitch (Quant only) carrying the largest 5-day run in the entire slate (+13.6%) — this is chasing an already-moved name, not entering a fresh thesis. Fundamentals explicitly warned against semis re-rating and was not overridden here because no other analyst stepped up. SMH already captures MU through its ETF holdings, so this adds correlation without adding conviction. At full slate, SMH+AMD+MU = 35% in one sub-sector riding the same AI-capex/memory-cycle thesis — that is a single-factor bet masquerading as three positions. MU is the weakest link and gets cut.

At 7% the downside is contained and the position is sized to reflect exactly the technical uncertainty. RSI 34 on a rotation-driven pullback is a credible dislocation signal for a franchise of this durability — Fundamentals is correct that this is not impairment. Non-correlated to semis, financials, or healthcare; adds genuine diversification to the slate. Toe-in sizing is appropriate given Technicals dissent.

risk.summary: The book's structural problem is semiconductor sub-sector stacking: SMH + AMD + MU = 35% in effectively one thesis (AI capex / memory cycle inflection) expressed three times with high cross-correlation and two names coming off extreme 5-day runs. Dropping MU (thinnest thesis, hottest recent move, fully redundant via SMH) and halving AMD (recency chase on top of SMH overlap) brings semiconductor exposure to ~21% with SMH doing the heavy lifting cleanly — no sector-limit breach, no chasing signal, and the AI-capex thesis remains fully represented. The remainder of the book — GS, UNH, GOOGL — is well-constructed with genuine theme diversification and appropriate sizing, and the revised slate lands at ~55% gross with 45% cash buffer.

trades.reason: Four-lens consensus with zero dissent is the highest conviction signal in the slate. Hawkish hold NIM tailwind is structurally intact and not consensus-priced; SpaceX IPO halo plus dealmaking renaissance provide independent catalysts even if IPO timing slips. RS +24 vs SPY with RSI 61 is clean momentum with room to extend. Maximum weight is earned — this is what maximum weight is for.

ETF structure is the correct vehicle for AI-capex secular exposure — eliminates binary single-name risk while capturing the dominant sector momentum regime. Confirmed 52wk-high breakout, RS +52, RSI 59 not extended. Three-analyst consensus, zero dissent. SMH is the semiconductor anchor; its full weight is what justifies cutting the single-name stacks below it.

Genuine diversifier — non-correlated to semis, rates, and mega-cap tech. Six institutional upgrades in two weeks mark a narrative inflection from utilization crisis to margin recovery with re-rating in early innings. RS +32.5, RSI 64 warm but not overbought, three analysts with zero dissent. June 23 dividend provides near-term sentiment anchor.

Momentum override of Fundamentals valuation caution is acceptable at half-size; at full-size it becomes undisciplined.

RSI 34 on a rotation-driven 10.6% pullback is a credible dislocation signal for a franchise with durable Search + YouTube + Cloud moats; Gemini deepens AI positioning. Sized at 7% to respect live Technicals dissent on mega-cap tech broadly. Adds genuine diversification with zero overlap to semis, financials, or healthcare. Toe-in sizing is the right call pending a technical base.
